#  About Subcontractor Invoices 
##### Using Owner or Specialty Contractor Terminology?
Procore can be configured to use terminology specific to General Contractors, Owners, or Specialty Contractors. Learn [how to apply the dictionary options. ](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-tool-names-and-terms-are-different-in-procore-for-general-contractors-owners-and-specialty-contractors)
  * To learn the differences: **Show/Hide**
    * This table shows the differences in tool names (**bold**) and terms across the point-of-view dictionaries for Project Financials.  
| **General Contractors  
English (United States) - Default**  | **Owners  
**** _English (Owner Terminology V2)_**  | **Specialty Contractors  
**** _English (Specialty Contractor Terminology)_**  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| **Invoicing**  | _**Invoicing**_  | _**Progress Billings**_  |  
| Owner  | _Funding_  | _Owner_  |  
| Owner/Client  | _Owner/Client_  | _GC/Client_  |  
| Prime Contract Change Order  | _Funding Change Order_  | _Client Contract Change Order_  |  
| **Prime Contracts**  | _**Funding**_  | _**Client Contracts**_  |  
| Revenue  | _Funding_  | _Revenue_  |  
| Subcontract  | _Contract_  | _Subcontract_  |  
| Subcontractor  | _Contractor_  | _Subcontractor_  |  
| Subcontractor Schedule of Values (SSOV)  | _Contractor Schedule of Values (CSOV)_  | _Subcontractor Schedule of Values (SSOV)_  |  
##### About These Dictionaries
      * **Default Setting:** The 'General Contractor' dictionary is enabled by default for all accounts.
      * **Availability:** These alternate dictionaries in _italics_ are available in US English only.  

##### How to Switch Your Dictionary
To change your company's terminology to the Owner or Specialty Contractor dictionary, contact your company's Procore Administrator. They will work with your Procore Point of Contact to make the change.


##  Background 
In Procore, a [subcontractor invoice](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) originates in the Project [Commitments](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/project-level/commitments) tool. Before you can create an invoice, your project team must create a commitment for the project, establish a billing period, and decide which internal users and/or external collaborators will be granted permission to submit new invoices.
#####  Tip
A Subcontractor Invoice is a request for payment submitted by a business or individual who has entered into a binding agreement with another contracting party. In Procore, a subcontractor invoice is an equivalent term for a payable invoice.
##  Prerequisites 
  * Decide which internal users and/or external collaborators can submit new invoices in the Procore project. See [Create Subcontractor Invoices](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/create-subcontractor-invoices).
  * Create a commitment for the invoice to bill against. See [Create](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/project-level/commitments/tutorials/create-a-commitment)[ Commitments](https://support.procore.com/products/online/user-guide/project-level/commitments/tutorials/create-a-commitment).
  * Create a billing period for the Procore project. See [Manage Billing Periods](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/manage-billing-periods).


##  Workflow 
This diagram summarizes subcontractor invoice creation.
![Diagram Invoicing Subcontractor Invoices](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/bc40f8ae1cdc8ac7612a50e5d34b3a0d/608d3e4e83ff7ddf4819d9e73ec52bba/Invoicing_-_Subcontractor_Invoices.png?fm=webp)
##  Steps 
  * Create Your Subcontractor Invoices in Procore
  * _Optional:_ Export Your Subcontractor Invoices to an Integrated ERP System


### Create Your Subcontractor Invoices in Procore
The steps for creating subcontractor invoices in Procore include:  
| Decide who will have permission to submit an invoice  | You have these choices:  
1. Grant [invoice submission rights](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-invoice-contacts) to [downstream collaborators](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms). In Procore, these users are called [invoice contacts](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-an-invoice-contact). 2. Gather paper or digital invoices from collaborators and grant invoice submission rights to your team. In Procore, these users are your [invoice administrators](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-invoice-administrators).  | [Considerations](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-do-we-need-to-consider-before-allowing-downstream-collaborators-to-submit-invoices-in-procore)  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| _Optional._ Decide if you want to collect signatures with DocuSign®  |  There are two (2) ways to collect signatures on commitments and invoices:
  * Use the Procore + DocuSign® integration. See [Enable the DocuSign® Integration on Your Company's Procore Account](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/enable-the-docusign-integration-on-your-companys-procore-account) and [Enable the DocuSign® Integration on a Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-project/tutorials/enable-or-disable-the-docusign-integration-on-a-procore-project).
  * Sign the digital or paper forms and upload them as attachments on the commitment and/or invoice.

 | [DocuSign®](https://support.procore.com/integrations/docusign)  |  
| Configure the Invoice Management tools  | Configure the invoicing settings in the Commitments and Invoicing tools. See [Configure Settings: Commitments](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-commitments) and [Configure Settings: Invoicing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/configure-settings-invoicing).  | [Commitments](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials) and [Invoicing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials)  |  
| Establish a billing period  | Create a billing period and place it in the _Open_ status. A billing period defines the start, end, and due date for submitting an invoice.  | [Manage Billing Periods](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/manage-billing-periods)  |  
| Create a commitment  | Subcontractor invoices originate from commitments. You can create multiple commitments for your collaborators in a Procore project. A commitment must be in the 'Approved' status before you can create an invoice.  | [Create a Commitment](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials)  |  
| _Optional:_ Grant invoice submission rights to downstream collaborators  | _Optional:_ An invoice administrator can grant invoice submission rights to downstream collaborators.  | [Add Invoice Contacts to a Purchase Order or Subcontract](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-invoice-contacts)  |  
| Configure the 'Advanced Settings' tab on the commitment  | For best results, configure the advanced settings before you add line items to the SOV and before you create invoices for the commitment.  | [Edit the Advanced Settings Tab on a Commitment](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/edit-the-advanced-settings-on-a-commitment)  |  
| Create invoices and submit them before the _Open_ billing period's 'Due Date'  | Learn the options for creating invoices with the Commitments tool. Only invoice administrators or invoice contacts on a commitment can create subcontractor invoices.  | [Create a Subcontractor Invoice](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/create-subcontractor-invoices)  |  
| Review submitted invoices  | Once submitted, an [invoice administrator](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) can review each line item on the invoice's Schedule of Values to approve or reject it.  | [Review a Subcontractor Invoice as an Invoice Administrator](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/review--approve-invoices)  |  
| Revise and resubmit invoices  | If an [invoice administrator](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) rejects one (1) or more invoice line items, users can make fixes and corrections until all the line items on the invoice's Schedule of Values are _Approved_.  | [Revise & Resubmit a Subcontractor Invoice as an Invoice Contact](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/revise-and-resubmit-a-subcontractor-invoice-as-an-invoice-contact)  |  
| _Optional._ Complete the signature process in DocuSign®  | Once _Approved_ , you can optionally complete the signature process in DocuSign®.  | [Complete Subcontractor Invoices with DocuSign®](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/complete-subcontractor-invoices-with-docusign)  |  
| _Optional:_ Sync invoices with an integrated ERP system  | You'll need to determine if your ERP connector supports subcontractor invoices.  | ERP Integrations  |  
## See Also
  * [Create a Subcontractor Schedule of Values (SSOV)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/create-a-subcontractor-schedule-of-values-ssov)
  * [Update a Subcontractor Schedule of Values as an Invoice Contact from the Invoicing Tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/update-a-subcontractor-schedule-of-values-as-an-invoice-contact-from-the-invoicing-tool)
  * [Create a Subcontractor Invoice on Behalf of an Invoice Contact](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/create-an-invoice-on-behalf-of-an-invoice-contact)


A _Procore Administrator_ is a user who has 'Admin' level permissions on all of the Company level Tools in Procore. Granting a user ‘Admin’ level permissions in the Company level Directory tool automatically assigns that user ‘Admin’ permissions on all Company level tools. Also called a _Company Administrator_.
title: About Subcontractor Invoices
description: Learn how to use the Project level Invoicing tool to manage your project's subcontractor invoices.
